blurb: App security on Apple platforms is built on mandatory code signing, sandboxing, and entitlement-based privilege control. Every app must be signed with a valid certificate, and the XNU kernel enforces code signing at launch and during execution. Apps are confined to a sandbox restricting access to system resources, other apps' data, and hardware interfaces – access is granted only through explicitly declared entitlements reviewed during App Store submission. The App Store review process provides automated and human inspection to detect policy violations, malware, and privacy abuses before apps reach users, supplemented by ongoing notarization checks post-publication.
